text.title: Language-Specific Mocking Tools
text.summary: Python unittest.mock/pytest-mock, JavaScript vi.mock/Jest, Go interface mocks. Common mistakes and how to avoid them.

section.title: Python: unittest.mock and pytest-mock
section.rule: Use mocker.patch() (pytest-mock) inside tests instead of @patch decorator — cleaner scope management.
Always use spec= or autospec=True to catch typos and attribute mismatches at call time.

antipattern.description: MagicMock without spec swallows typos silently
antipattern.code:
# Bad: mock.notexistingmethod() returns a Mock instead of raising AttributeError mock = MagicMock() result = mock.notexistingmethod() # no error — silent false positive
# Good: autospec raises AttributeError for methods not on the real class mock = create_autospec(PaymentGateway) result = mock.notexistingmethod() # AttributeError caught immediately
antipattern.reason: autospec validates call signatures against the real class at mock-creation time.

antipattern.code:
# Bad: patches the original module, not where it's used mocker.patch("stripe.Charge.create")
# Good: patch where the name is looked up (the importing module) mocker.patch("myapp.services.payment.stripe.Charge.create")
antipattern.reason: Python looks up names at call time in the namespace of the importing module.

section.title: JavaScript: Vitest and Jest
section.rule: Use vi.mock() / jest.mock() at the module level — hoisted above imports by the bundler.
Use vi.spyOn() for partial mocking of a single method on a real object.
Always call vi.clearAllMocks() or jest.clearAllMocks() in afterEach to prevent state leakage.

section.title: Go: Interface-Based Mocking
section.rule: Go mocking is interface substitution — define a minimal interface, implement it in tests.
Use mockery for auto-generating mocks from interfaces; use manual mocks for simple cases.
